title: polypodium pilosella
intro: Polypodium pilosella, also known as the hair-stalked polypody, is a type of fern species belonging to the Polypodiaceae family. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The health benefits of Polypodium pilosella include its potential to treat wounds, ulcers, and skin conditions, as well as its use as an anti-pyretic to reduce fever. Herbal preparations of Polypodium pilosella are typically made by infusing the leaves in hot water or mixing them with other herbs to create tonics and poultices for topical application.
